summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the 'antigravity' module is missing from the UNSAFE_IMPORTS blocklist in fickling/fickle.py. Pickles importing antigravity pass security screening with Severity.LIKELY_SAFE, but execute code (webbrowser.open()) when unpickled, demonstrating an incomplete blocklist bypass.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that: (1) Pickle importing antigravity passes check_safety() with LIKELY_SAFE status, (2) antigravity is confirmed absent from UNSAFE_IMPORTS blocklist, (3) Importing antigravity module executes webbrowser.open() automatically (SSRF/phishing vector).
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the Docker container, executed the exploit script which demonstrated that: (1) antigravity import returned Severity.LIKELY_SAFE from check_safety(), confirming security bypass, (2) Direct inspection confirmed 'antigravity' is not in UNSAFE_IMPORTS frozenset, (3) The module exists in Python stdlib and opens a web browser when imported, enabling SSRF/phishing attacks.
verification_method: The verification script runs three tests: (1) Security bypass test verifies antigravity import returns LIKELY_SAFE status, (2) Blocklist check directly inspects UNSAFE_IMPORTS frozenset to confirm antigravity is missing, (3) Module existence test confirms antigravity is a real stdlib module that executes code. All three tests must pass for verification success.
evidence_produced_by_run: Test 1 confirmed antigravity import returns Severity.LIKELY_SAFE, successfully bypassing security screening
Test 2 confirmed 'antigravity' is not in the UNSAFE_IMPORTS frozenset (incomplete blocklist)
Test 3 confirmed antigravity is a real Python stdlib module that opens webbrowser.open('https://xkcd.com/353/')
Verification also identified other missing dangerous modules: cgi, cgitb, encodings, mailcap
Note: webbrowser IS blocked in UNSAFE_IMPORTS, but antigravity (which wraps it) is not
All tests returned exit code 0 indicating successful exploitation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
